idea.topic: All planets fit between Earth and Moon
idea.hook: Is the space between Earth and the Moon truly vast?
facts: The average distance from Earth to our Moon is approximately 384,400 kilometers.
The combined diameters of all 8 planets (Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune) sum up to about 380,010 kilometers.
This includes Jupiter (139,820 km), Saturn (116,460 km), Uranus (50,724 km), Neptune (49,244 km), Venus (12,104 km), Mars (6,779 km), and Mercury (4,879 km).
idea.payoff: All eight planets in our Solar System could fit perfectly side-by-side in the gap between Earth and its Moon!
idea.title: Earth-Moon Gap: Planet-Sized Surprise! 🤯🌕

scenes.text: Is the space between Earth and the Moon truly vast?
scenes.visual: Starts with Earth and Moon as small, distant spheres in a seemingly endless dark space, subtle stars in background.
Earth and Moon slowly zoom towards the viewer, separating slightly to reveal a vast, dark gap between them. A digital measurement line appears.

scenes.text: Combined diameter of all 8 planets: ~380,010 km
scenes.visual: The four gas giants (Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune) slide in from the left, lining up side-by-side, still smaller than the Earth-Moon gap previously shown.

scenes.text: That includes Jupiter (139,820 km) + Saturn (116,460 km)...
scenes.visual: The remaining terrestrial planets (Earth, Venus, Mars, Mercury) slide in from the right, completing the lineup of all 8 planets. The entire chain of planets is now visible.

scenes.text: All 8 planets fit perfectly between Earth & Moon!
scenes.visual: Earth appears on the left, the Moon on the right. The entire line of 8 planets precisely fills the space between them, shrinking the perceived gap. A satisfying 'click' or 'snap' visual effect as they fit.
